reasoning: The US Treasury yield curve is upward sloping (normal) and rising, which signifies market expectations of future economic expansion [57205ed3e4b4] and indicates expectations of economic expansion [98b8f9c4befb]. Simultaneously, the 10-year real interest rate is low relative to history (19th percentile), which increases liquidity and reduces volatility in risk assets [99ad6cdde172], leads to expansionary GDP [2328dabf508c], and allows equity indices to command higher PE valuation multiples [5e068f4bde46]. The VIX is in a normal regime and broadly flat, consistent with the low-volatility environment created by low real rates. All three indicators align to support a bullish outlook for equities.
